expect: Auto-discovery of STRUCTURAL tools (>=2 unique L-NNN refs) replaces hardcoded list. Enforcement rate stable at ~28.6%. Future tool additions auto-tracked. Falsified if auto-discovery produces >5% different results than manual curation.
actual: Auto-discovery implemented: _auto_discover_structural_files() scans tools/ for .py/.sh with >=2 unique L-NNN refs. Excludes archive/ and test_ files. min_refs=2 discovers 43 files, producing 76 STRUCTURAL lessons = exact match with manual curation (76). Enforcement rate 28.6% (identical). 7 tools newly discovered that were never in hardcoded list (compact.py, domain_map.py, eval_sufficiency.py, lesson_quality_fixer.py, maintenance_common.py, meta_tooler.py, orient_state.py). L-1069 updated with S472 replication.
diff: Expected rate stable at ~28.6%: CONFIRMED at 28.6% (exact match). Expected <5% divergence from manual: CONFIRMED at 0.0% divergence. Key finding: min_refs=3 was too strict (25.6%, missed 8 lessons). min_refs=2 recovers all manually-curated tools. The auto-discovery IS a structural improvement to the swarming mechanism — L-601 no longer applies to the enforcement tracker scope. Colony session 8/10.
self_apply: Auto-discovering enforcement scope IS enforcement improvement — the discovery mechanism structurally prevents the very decay it measures (L-601 applied to L-601 tracker)
